---
title: "Decision Making Statistics — S04"
subtitle: "2nd Year Business School | ESSCA"
author: "M. Kachour"
lang: en
date-modified: 2026-05-31
---

# Welcome

This course introduces **inferential statistics** for 2nd-year business school students. It covers the mathematical foundations needed to move from descriptive statistics to data-driven decision making under uncertainty.

## Course Structure

The course is organized in **15 sessions** across three main parts:

| # | Session | Topics Covered |
|---|---------|----------------|
| 1 | [Sets & Combinatorics](sessions/session-01.qmd) | Sets, combinatorial analysis |
| 2 | [Probability](sessions/session-02.qmd) | Calculation of probabilities |
| 3 | [Discrete Random Variables](sessions/session-03.qmd) | Discrete distributions |
| 4 | [Continuous Random Variables](sessions/session-04.qmd) | Continuous distributions |
| 5 | [Practical Work — Distributions](sessions/session-05.qmd) | Applying distributions in Excel |
| 6 | [Data Description](sessions/session-06.qmd) | Numerical summaries, reminders |
| 7 | [Estimation](sessions/session-07.qmd) | Confidence intervals |
| 8 | [Conformity Tests](sessions/session-08.qmd) | Hypothesis testing — conformity |
| 9 | [Comparison Tests](sessions/session-09.qmd) | Hypothesis testing — comparison |
| 10 | [Practical Work — Inference](sessions/session-10.qmd) | Inference in Excel |
| 11 | [ANOVA Test](sessions/session-11.qmd) | Analysis of variance |
| 12 | [Chi-Square Test](sessions/session-12.qmd) | Goodness-of-fit, independence |
| 13 | [Linear Correlation Test](sessions/session-13.qmd) | Correlation coefficient test |
| 14 | [Practical Work — Tests](sessions/session-14.qmd) | Advanced tests in Excel |
| 15 | [Mock Exam](sessions/session-15.qmd) | Revision & mock exam |

**Part I** — Probability Foundations (1–5) · **Part II** — Statistical
Inference (6–10) · **Part III** — Advanced Tests (11–15).

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this course, students will be able to:

1. Apply combinatorial analysis and probability rules to real-world problems
2. Work with discrete and continuous probability distributions (Binomial, Poisson, Normal)
3. Compute and interpret confidence intervals for means and proportions
4. Formulate, perform, and interpret statistical hypothesis tests
5. Choose the appropriate test for a given business problem
6. Use Excel to automate statistical computations

## Course Materials

Each session folder contains:

- **Animation material** — step-by-step lecture slides
- **Summary** — key formulas and concepts
- **Exercises** — problems to practice during and after the session
- **Answered exercises** — complete solutions

## Recommended Literature

<!-- Book profile: Author(s) | Title | Edition | Publisher/Source -->

| Author(s) | Title | Edition | Publisher/Source |
|-----------|-------|---------|------------------|
| Kachour, M. | *Inferential Statistics — Course Notes (MS04-001-G_04_EN_2023)* | N/A | ESSCA |
| Newbold, P.; Carlson, W.; Thorne, B. | *Statistics for Business and Economics* | 9th | Pearson |
| Anderson, D.; Sweeney, D.; Williams, T. | *Statistics for Business and Economics* | 14th | Cengage |
